english: ter solstice; dgun-nyi-ldog-gi tig (dgun nyi ldog gi thig), or kor-tig (khor thig) the tropic of Capricorn Cs. (cf. the remark at the end of dgun (dgun)); dgun-stód (dgun stod), dgun-smád (dgun smad) the first and the last half of winter, (v. dus (dus)).

three different spellings of the same root, all of them pronounced *gur*, crooked, dbyibs-dgur (dbyibs dgur) of crooked stature S.g.; rgur zìg (rgur zhig) stoop down! bend your back! Dzl.; sgúr-te (sgur te) writhing (with pain) Dzl.; sgúr-po (sgur po) crooked, hump-backed, by birth Lt.; with age Thgy.; C. col. *gur-gúr* id.; mgo dgúr-ba (mgo dgur ba) to duck, to bend vb.n.; to submit, to humble one's self (cf. dgú-ba (dgu ba)). Cs.: dgúr-po (dgur po), dgúr-mo (dgur mo) a crooked man, a crooked woman; tsigs-dgúr (tshigs dgur) a crooked back, crook-backed; lag-dgúr (lag dgur) having crooked hands etc.; dgur-'gro (dgur 'gro) of a stooping gait.
1. instr. of dgu (dgu). - 2. C., W., this day five days (the present day included).

1. happiness, welfare; happy, propitious, dgé-źiṅ s̀is-pa (dge zhing shis pa) Wth. More frq.: 2. virtue (opp. to mi-dgé-ba (mi dge ba), and sdig-pa (sdig pa)), also adj. virtuous, sems dgé-ba (sems dge ba) a virtuous mind Glr., las dgé-ba (las dge ba), mi-dgé-ba (mi dge ba) good and bad actions Stg.; dgé-bai rtsá-ba (dge ba'i rtsa ba) roots of virtue, meritorious actions, from which afterwards the fruits of reward come forth; dgé-rtsa skyéd-pa (dge rtsa skyed pa) frq., spyód-pa (spyod pa) Thgy., byéd-pa (byed pa) Mil. to produce such a root, to achieve a meritorious action; dgé-ba séms-par 'gyúr-ba (dge ba sems par 'gyur ba) to become inclined to virtue, i.e. converted Dzl.; dge-tsógs (dge tshogs) (v. tsogs (tshogs)) a virtuous work, a good deed; dgé-ba bcu (dge ba bcu) the ten virtues, viz. 1. srog mi gcód-pa (srog mi gcod pa), not to kill anything living (by which Buddhism has replaced our scriptural interdiction of murder); 2. ma byín-par mi lén-pa (ma byin par mi len pa) not to take what has not been given (those who closely stick to the word go even so far, that they will not touch or accept an alms, unless it be put into their hands); 3. lóg-par mi yyém-pa (g.yem pa) not to fornicate; 4. rdzun mi smrá-ba (rdzun mi smra ba) not to tell a lie; 5. tsig-rtsúb mi smrá-ba (tshig rtsub mi smra ba) not to abuse or revile; 6. ṅag-kyál (ngag kyal) (or °kyál ('kyal)) mi smrá-ba (mi smra ba) not to talk foolishness (cf. kyál-ka (kyal ka)); 7. prá-ma mi byéd-pa (phra ma mi byed pa) not to calumniate; 8. brnáb-sems mi byéd pa (brnab sems mi byed pa) not to be avaricious or covetous; 9. gnód-sems mi byéd-pa (gnod sems mi byed pa) not to think upon doing harm or mischief; 10. lóg-lta mi byéd-pa (log lta mi byed pa) not to entertain heretic notions, or positively, yáṅ-dag-par ltá-ba (yang dag par lta ba) Stg. to be orthodox. - 3. fasting, abstinence, in the phrase: dgé-ba srúṅ-ba (dge ba srung ba) to fast, to abstain from food, frq. - 4. alms, charity; banquet, treat, as a religious work, s̀i-dge gsón-dge (shi dge gson dge) largesses, treats, taking place at funerals, or given in one's life time Mil. (W. *yá-tra*, and *ku-rím*). Comp. and deriv. dge-bskos (dge bskos) censor, and at the same time provost and beadle in a monastery, who has to watch over strict order, and to punish the transgressors Köpp. II. 259, 276; in Ld. he is also called c̈os-krims-pa (chos khrims pa) (vulg. *c̈osrimpa*). - dge-rgán (dge rgan) surety, moral bail, a monk that is made answerable for the moral conduct of an other, who is placed under his care and called dge-gźón (dge gzhon); also in a gen. sense: teacher, schoolmaster. - dge-bsnyén (dge bsnyen), fem. dge-bsnyén-ma (dge bsnyen ma) (Ssk. उपासक and उपासिका) 1. the pious of the laymen who retaining their secular occupations have renounced the five cardinal sins (murder, theft, fornication, lying, and drunkenness) and provide for the maintenance of the priests (so in Dzl. and gen. in the earlier writings). 2. in later times as much as a novice, probationer, catechumen, i.e. either a kind of clerical apprentice (the Shabi of the Mongols, srâmanera Ssk., v. Köpp. II., 252), or one of a next higher degree, a candidate (v. Schl. 162). - dge-ltás (dge ltas) S.g. a propitious omen, a favourable prognostic. - dge-dún (dge 'dun) (col. *gen-dún*), prop. dge-slóṅ-gi °dun (dge slong gi 'dun) (Burn. II., 435) Ssk. संघ, the whole body of the clergy, priesthood; dge-°dun-dkon-mc̈óg (dge 'dun dkon mchog) the priesthood as one of the
sanskrit: शुभ, कुशल, श्रेयस्; also स्वस्ति, कल्याणं, seldom पुण्य
